# {{ Document Title }}

## Executive Summary

Provide a concise overview of the document.

Summarize:

- What this document is about
- Why it exists
- Who should read it
- Expected outcome

This section should be understandable without reading the remainder of the document.

---

# Purpose

Describe why this document exists.

Include:

- Business purpose
- Technical purpose
- Problem being addressed
- Expected value

---

# Scope

Clearly define what is included in this document.

### In Scope

- Item
- Item
- Item

### Out of Scope

- Item
- Item
- Item

---

# Background

Provide the context required to understand this document.

Possible topics include:

- Business context
- Existing solution
- Current challenges
- Assumptions
- Historical decisions

---

# Goals

Define the objectives this document intends to achieve.

Example:

- Goal 1
- Goal 2
- Goal 3

Goals should be measurable whenever possible.

---

# Non-Goals

Explicitly define what this document does **not** attempt to solve.

Example:

- Not a deployment guide
- Not an implementation guide
- Not a security specification

---

# Audience

Identify the intended readers.

Example:

| Audience | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| Product Managers | Product planning |
| Architects | Technical design |
| Developers | Implementation |
| QA Engineers | Validation |
| DevOps Engineers | Deployment |
| Security Engineers | Governance |
| Technical Writers | Documentation |

---

# Definitions

Define important terminology used throughout the document.

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| EAEP | Enterprise AI Engineering Platform |
| MCP | Model Context Protocol |
| TBD | To Be Defined |

Avoid introducing undefined acronyms.

---

# Functional Requirements

Describe the functional capabilities.

| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| FR-001 | Requirement | High |
| FR-002 | Requirement | Medium |

Each requirement should be:

- Clear
- Testable
- Unambiguous

---

# Non-Functional Requirements

Document quality attributes.

Example categories:

| Category | Requirement |

| Performance | TBD |
| Availability | TBD |
| Scalability | TBD |

| Security | TBD |

---

# Design Considerations

Describe important design decisions and constraints.

Possible topics:

- Architecture
- Interfaces
- Integration
- Data flow
- Dependencies
- Scalability
- Security
- Extensibility

---

# Assumptions

List assumptions made while preparing this document.

Example:

- External APIs remain available.
- Infrastructure supports Kubernetes.
- Authentication service already exists.

---

# Constraints

Document known limitations.

Examples:

- Budget
- Timeline
- Technology
- Compliance
- Organizational

---

# Risks

Identify potential risks.

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| Risk | High | Mitigation |

---

# Dependencies

Document dependencies on other systems or documents.

Examples:

- Product Vision
- High-Level Architecture
- Security Architecture
- API Specifications

---

# Success Criteria

Define how success will be measured.

Examples:

- Documentation approved.
- Requirements validated.
- Stakeholders aligned.
- Implementation completed successfully.

---

# Future Enhancements

Capture potential future improvements.

Example:

- Enhancement 1
- Enhancement 2
- Enhancement 3

---

# Open Questions

Capture unresolved items.

| ID | Question | Owner | Status |
|----|----------|-------|--------|
| Q-001 | Question | TBD | Open |

# References

List external references if applicable.

Examples:

- Standards
- RFCs
- Microsoft Documentation
- OpenAI Documentation
- Internal Design Documents
